LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SHERMAN LEE MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT SHERMAN LEE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH A PROOF OF SERVICE BY A REGISTERED PROCESS SERVER CREATES A PRESUMPTION OF PROPER SERVICE, THE COURT CREDITS MR. LEE'S UNCONTROVERTED DECLARATION THAT HE DOES NOT KNOW THE PERSON WITH WHOM THE SUMMONS WAS LEFT AND SUCH A PERSON DOES NOT LIVE AT THE ADDRESS INDICATED AND THAT MR. LEE DID NOT RECEIVE THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT. (LEE DECL. 5-7; SEE FERNANDES V. SINGH (2017) 16 CAL.APP.5TH 932, 941["DECLARATION OF NON-SERVICE IF CREDITED BY THE TRIAL COURT CAN REBUT THE PRESUMPTION OF PROPER SERVICE"].) BECAUSE SERVICE PAPERS WERE NOT LEFT WITH "A COMPETENT MEMBER OF THE HOUSEHOLD" OF MR. LEE AND SERVICE WAS THUS IMPROPER, THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS VACATED AS VOID. (SEE ELLARD V. CONWAY (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 540, 544 ["UNDER SECTION 473, SUBDIVISION (D), THE COURT MAY SET ASIDE A DEFAULT JUDGMENT WHICH IS VALID ON ITS FACE, BUT VOID, AS A MATTER OF LAW, DUE TO IMPROPER SERVICE."].) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 64379897)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2019 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Set For Trial On Nov-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHERMAN LEE MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT

Defendant Sherman Lee's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. Although a proof of service by a registered process server creates a presumption of proper service, the court credits Mr. Lee's uncontroverted declaration that he does not know the person with whom the summons was left and such a person does not live at the address indicated and that Mr. Lee did not receive the summons and complaint. (Lee Decl. 5-7; see Fernandes v. Singh (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 932, 941["declaration of non-service if credited by the trial court can rebut the presumption of proper service"].) Because service papers were not left with "a competent member of the household" of Mr. Lee and service was thus improper, the default judgment is vacated as void. (See Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544 ["Under section 473, subdivision (d), the court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Off Calendar
